raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say 'make this stronger', should I prioritize (A) the technical argument (logic, evidence, novelty) or (B) the writing and presentation (clarity, framing, flow)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request to make the work 'stronger' implies sharpening the core contribution claim and its framing.",
    "R1.2": "A 'stronger' argument requires verifying the logical chain from the method's properties to its claimed optimality.",
    "R1.3": "Making the paper 'stronger' requires ensuring the empirical performance claims are fully supported by the reported results.",
    "R1.4": "Strengthening the paper involves clearly articulating its novelty compared to existing baselines and related work.",
    "R1.6": "The request implies improving sentence-level clarity and persuasiveness to create a 'stronger' narrative.",
    "RX.1": "A 'stronger' scientific contribution honestly discloses any uncertainties or limitations of the proposed method.",
    "RX.2": "Strengthening the paper involves scrutinizing strong assertions like 'unique cost-optimal policy' to avoid overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "The work will be 'stronger' if all claims and discussions remain tightly focused on the core problem defined.",
    "RX.4": "Part of making a research artifact 'stronger' is considering and mitigating potential negative downstream consequences.",
    "RX.5": "A 'stronger' paper is built on traceable scholarship and clear attribution of its intellectual components."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say 'make this stronger', should I prioritize (A) the technical argument (logic, evidence, novelty) or (B) the writing and presentation (clarity, framing, flow)?

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the work 'stronger' implies sharpening the core contribution claim and its framing.
rationale.R1.2: A 'stronger' argument requires verifying the logical chain from the method's properties to its claimed optimality.
rationale.R1.3: Making the paper 'stronger' requires ensuring the empirical performance claims are fully supported by the reported results.
rationale.R1.4: Strengthening the paper involves clearly articulating its novelty compared to existing baselines and related work.
rationale.R1.6: The request implies improving sentence-level clarity and persuasiveness to create a 'stronger' narrative.
rationale.RX.1: A 'stronger' scientific contribution honestly discloses any uncertainties or limitations of the proposed method.
rationale.RX.2: Strengthening the paper involves scrutinizing strong assertions like 'unique cost-optimal policy' to avoid overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: The work will be 'stronger' if all claims and discussions remain tightly focused on the core problem defined.
rationale.RX.4: Part of making a research artifact 'stronger' is considering and mitigating potential negative downstream consequences.
rationale.RX.5: A 'stronger' paper is built on traceable scholarship and clear attribution of its intellectual components.
